title: System and method for voice recognition using a peripheral device
abstract: A system and method for dictation using a peripheral device includes a voice recognition mouse. The voice recognition mouse includes a microphone, a first button, a processor coupled to the microphone and the first button, and a memory coupled to the processor. The memory stores instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to detect actuation of the first button and in response to detecting actuation of the first button, invoke the microphone for capturing audio speech from a user. The captured audio speech is streamed to a first module. The first module is configured to invoke a second module for converting the captured audio speech into text and forward the text to the first module for providing to an application expecting the text, the application being configured to display the text on a display device.
claims: 1. A computer mouse comprising: a microphone; a processor coupled to the microphone; and a memory coupled to the processor, wherein the memory stores instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: detect a selection for invoking the microphone; capture audio speech from a user via the microphone; transmit the audio speech to a first client device, wherein the first client device is configured to transmit a request to convert the audio speech into first text, receive the first text corresponding to the audio speech, and provide the first text as input to a first application; and transmit the audio speech to a second client device configured to convert the audio speech into second text and provide the second text as input to a second application.
